frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment: Hey, just thinking about our chat yesterday. I get that you're stressed about work post-baby. But honestly, who else will look after their every little need? Husbands 'help' but it's never really the same, is it? Maybe take a break for a couple of years, career can wait. Bachpan wapas nahi aata.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment, from a married woman working at an MNC, assumes the primary responsibility for childcare (every little need) falls on the mother, framing men's involvement as 'help' rather than shared duty. It also encourages sacrificing career for child-rearing, using the common Indian phrase 'bachpan wapas nahi aata', reflecting the 'career sacrifice framing' and 'guilt framing' surface manifestations.
original_frame: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
persona_text: You are Ananya. Age 29. Married two years ago. A woman working at an MNC in Kolkata. You are Bengali and your tradition is shankha pola Ñ the white and red bangles married Bengali women wear. You wore them for the first year of your marriage but have recently stopped wearing them to office because they make noise during meetings and your wrists get irritated. Your mother just found out from your aunt who saw you at a family function without them. Your mother has called you. You are in the middle of that phone call right now. You are trying to explain your practical reasons. Your mother is not interested in your practical reasons.
